question.text: Income from commodity futures settled through delivery is treated as:

answer.text: Business income arising from the purchase/sale of commodities — not as derivative trading income

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When commodity futures are settled through physical delivery, the transaction is treated as a business purchase/sale of the commodity — not as derivative trading income. **The Trap:** Not capital gains or necessarily speculative. Not tax-free. Physical delivery changes the characterization from derivative to commodity transaction. **Cross-Exam Context:** Tax classification based on settlement mode is specific to commodity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Taking delivery through futures may have better tax treatment than booking derivative P&L — consult with a CA to optimize your tax position based on settlement choices.
